summary: BUG FIX VERIFIED: Edit modal input lag/unresponsive issue in Admin Dashboard is FIXED. All edit modals (Cities, Airports, Hotels, Transfers) now accept text input correctly without lag or focus loss. The fix involved converting nested EditModal component to renderEditModal function and using useCallback for handleFieldChange.

bug_fix_verification.issue: Edit modal input fields losing focus and becoming unresponsive after typing
bug_fix_verification.root_cause: EditModal was defined as nested component inside AdminDashboard, causing React to recreate component on every state change
bug_fix_verification.fix_applied: Converted to render function (renderEditModal) and used useCallback for handleFieldChange

edit_city_modal.typing_test: PASSED - Typed 'TestCity123' without lag or focus loss

edit_airport_modal.name_field: PASSED - Typed 'Test Airport Name 123' without lag

edit_hotel_modal.name_field: PASSED - Typed 'Test Hotel Resort & Spa 2026' without lag

edit_transfer_modal.title_field: PASSED - Typed 'Test Private Transfer Title' without lag
edit_transfer_modal.to_location: PASSED - Typed 'Burj Al Arab Hotel' without issues

add_new_city.modal_opens: PASSED - Modal opens with empty fields
add_new_city.save_works: PASSED - City saved successfully and appears in list

critical_code_review_comments: Console warning: 'In HTML, <tr> cannot be a child of <span>' - React hydration warning in Proposals table rendering. This is cosmetic and does not affect functionality.

success_rate.frontend: 100% - All edit modal input tests passed

seed_data_creation: Created TEST_NewTestCity/TEST_Country city for testing Add functionality
context_for_next_testing_agent: Edit modal input lag bug is FIXED. All input fields in Cities, Airports, Hotels, and Transfers edit modals now work correctly. Access Admin Dashboard via 'Backend Admin Dashboard' link in footer after login. The fix uses renderEditModal function (not nested component) and useCallback for handleFieldChange to prevent unnecessary re-renders.
test_navigation_steps: 2. Scroll to footer and click 'Backend Admin Dashboard'
3. Click on any tab (Cities, Airports, Hotels, Transfers)
4. Hover over an item card and click Edit icon
5. Type in any input field - text should appear without lag
